revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:平濱八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name =平濱八幡宮 |image =File:Hirahamahachimanguhonden.JPG |location ={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}八幡町303 |coordinates ={{Coord|35.441667|N|133.116806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity ={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chuai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}} |Rank ={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |main hall = 流造 |札所等 ={{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Route|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Route|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395860}}13番 |festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|qid=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} |festival = {{ill|Takeuchi Shrine Grand festival|ja|武内神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Shrine Grand festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578979|1=Takeuchi Shrine Grand festival}} |prefecture={{ill|Matsue|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} }} '''Hirahama Hachimangu''' (ひらはまはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}. The former {{ill|shakaku|ja|Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階数|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766628}} was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. It is the 13th station on the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Route|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Route|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395860}}. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chuai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}}. == History == The date of establishment is unknown, but there is a record that in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ten'ei|en|Ten'ei|qid=Q1193915|lt=Ten'ei|1=Ten'ei}} (1111), the {{ill|Onmyoryo|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyoryo|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} divined the date for the shrine's {{ill|Sengū|en|Sengū|qid=Q2271023|lt=Sengu|1=Sengū}}, so it must have been founded before that. It is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine in {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This land was a territory of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q740246}}, and the shrine was called "Hirahama Betsugu," with a branch of the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} of Iwashimizu Hachimangu serving the shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, the shrine buildings were constructed by the {{ill|Kamei clan|ja|亀井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamei clan|1=Kamei clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370753}}, {{ill|Amago clan|en|Amago clan|qid=Q2575609|lt=Amago clan|1=Amago clan}}, and {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mori clan|1=Mōri clan}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was directly controlled by the lords of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|qid=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}. In the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} (1942), it was Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == Facilities == The precinct shrine {{ill|Takeuchi Shrine|ja|武内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Shrine|1=Takeuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24862515}} enshrines {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and is affectionately called "Takeuchi-san." Due to Takeuchi no Sukune's longevity, he is worshipped as a god of longevity. Other precinct shrines include: *{{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}) *{{ill|Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Jingū|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}) *{{ill|Kawara Shrine|ja|加和羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} and eleven other deities) *{{ill|Higashi Zuijin Shrine|ja|東随神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Zuijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578982|1=Higashi Zuijin Shrine}} and {{ill|Nishi Zuijin Shrine|ja|西随神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Zuijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578985|1=Nishi Zuijin Shrine}} ({{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi no Mikoto|ja|豊盤間戸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tokotachi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kushibama no Mikoto|ja|櫛磐間戸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushibama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) As an external shrine, there is {{ill|Kokura Shrine|ja|高良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Shrine|1=kourajinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124481860}} ({{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉垂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578988|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}). == festivals == On August 31, there is the grand festival of the precinct shrine Takeuchi Shrine, attracting many visitors until late at night. The main festival on September 15 features a divine procession. == Access == * Take a {{ill|city bus|ja|市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公営バス|1=public bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16099346}} from the No. 5 stop at {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|qid=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} towards {{ill|Takeya|ja|竹矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or take the {{ill|Ichibata Bus|ja|一畑バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichibata Bus|1=Ichibata Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11352955}} towards {{ill|Arashima Station|en|Arashima Station|qid=Q4784373|lt=Arashima Station|1=Arashima Station}}, and get off at the {{ill|Takeuchi Shrine Mae Bus Stop|ja|武内神社前バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Shrine Mae Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, eds. ''Shinshū Fukyūban Jinja Jiten''. 1997. Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. ISBN 4-490-10474-X == External Links == *[https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/takeuchijinja/ Hirahama Hachimangu] (Izumo Province Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Route) {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shimane Prefecture}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[ja:平濱八幡宮]] {{translated page|ja|平濱八幡宮|version=102053648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11483250}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsue]] [[Category:Former_Prefectural_Shrines_in_Shimane_Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Matsue]] [[Category:Izumo Shrine and Buddhism Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
